title: centaurea kunkelii
intro: Centaurea kunkelii, also known as Kunkel's knapweed, is a perennial plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Centaurea kunkelii can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in topical applications such as salves or creams.
